name: Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop
name_original: Villafuerte desde Curiel del Duero – circular por Baltanas y Antigüedad
description: This difficult 79.9-mile (128.5 km) road cycling route from Curiel del Duero offers a challenging ride through Castilian landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling around Curiel De Duero features routes through the Duero Valley, characterized by rolling hills and historic castles. The terrain often includes gentle ascents and descents, suitable for various fitness levels. The region's landscape is shaped by the Duero River, offering scenic views along its banks and through agricultural areas. Best road cycling routes around Curiel De Duero The most popular road cycling route is Cogeces del Monte from Peñafiel – circular through the Duero Valley, a 33.1 miles (53.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular journey through the scenic Duero Valley. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Peñafiel and Encinas de Esgueva Castles Loop, a moderate 40.7 miles (65.5 km) path. This route connects several historic castles, providing cultural points of interest. Local road cyclists also love the Peñafiel and Curiel de Duero Castles Loop, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail leading through the local castle landscapes, often completed in about 51 minutes. Road cycling around Curiel De Duero is defined by routes through the Duero Valley, past historic castles, and across rolling agricultural lands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Curiel De Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Curiel De Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Curiel De Duero?
A: Curiel De Duero offers a variety of road cycling routes suitable for different skill levels. You'll find &lt;strong&gt;10 easy routes&lt;/strong&gt; perfect for a relaxed ride, &lt;strong&gt;7 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt; for those seeking a bit more challenge, and &lt;strong&gt;1 difficult route&lt;/strong&gt; for experienced cyclists looking for a demanding journey.

Q: What are the typical lengths of road cycling routes in Curiel De Duero?
A: Route lengths vary significantly, catering to different preferences. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169229644/penafiel-and-curiel-de-duero-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel and Curiel de Duero Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter 11.2 miles (18.0 km) ride, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169184353/penafiel-and-encinas-de-esgueva-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel and Encinas de Esgueva Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to 40.7 miles (65.5 km). For a truly epic ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169243322/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers approximately 79.8 miles (128.5 km).

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Curiel De Duero has &lt;strong&gt;10 easy road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt;. A great option for a more relaxed pace is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169229644/penafiel-and-curiel-de-duero-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel and Curiel de Duero Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.2 miles (18.0 km) long and takes about 51 minutes to complete, offering scenic views of local castles.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a significant challenge, there is &lt;strong&gt;1 difficult route&lt;/strong&gt; available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169243322/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 79.8 miles (128.5 km) ride with over 1,100 meters of elevation gain, connecting various historic castles and offering extensive views of the Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Curiel De Duero?
A: The best times for road cycling in Curiel De Duero are typically &lt;strong&gt;spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October)&lt;/strong&gt;. During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be quite hot, especially in July and August, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended if cycling during those months.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: The routes in Curiel De Duero are characterized by the beautiful Duero Valley landscape. You'll cycle through &lt;strong&gt;rolling hills, past historic castles, and across agricultural lands&lt;/strong&gt;. Many routes offer scenic views along the banks of the Duero River, providing a mix of natural beauty and cultural landmarks.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Curiel De Duero?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Curiel De Duero are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169195179/cogeces-del-monte-from-penafiel-circular-through-the-duero-valley" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cogeces del Monte from Peñafiel – circular through the Duero Valley&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a complete loop through the scenic region.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Curiel De Duero is rich in history, and many routes pass by impressive landmarks. You can expect to see several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2728143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5507727" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Curiel de Duero Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169184353/penafiel-and-encinas-de-esgueva-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñafiel and Encinas de Esgueva Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically connects multiple historic castles, offering cultural points of interest throughout your ride.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Curiel De Duero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt;. Road cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the beautiful Duero Valley scenery, and the opportunity to explore historic castles. The well-maintained routes and varying difficulty levels also contribute to a positive experience for many riders.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest related to the Duero River?
A: Yes, the Duero River is a central feature of the landscape. Many routes offer scenic views along its banks. You might also encounter structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5617069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wooden Bridge over the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the Senda del Duero, providing picturesque crossing points and photo opportunities.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Curiel De Duero?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;18 road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Curiel De Duero, offering a wide range of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route in the area?
A: The longest road cycling route in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169243322/ribera-del-duero-and-esgueva-valley-castles-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribera del Duero and Esgueva Valley Castles Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 79.8 miles (128.5 km) and features significant elevation changes, making it a challenging and rewarding ride.
tips: Before reaching the town of Curriel del Duero you can see in the background the castle that dominates the whole of the town and that rises on top of a stone. The panorama is spectacular, imagining how the castle was built in previous centuries.
A small town on the Duero River, the town of Pesquera de Duero lies north of Peñafiel
Fortification dating from the 15th century where the Franco family lived, a converted Jewish family from Toledo.
Baltanás is a small town famous for the underground wine storage cellars that can be found scattered up a hill in the town.

Small town that is located on the plain called El Cerrato and which generates that the surroundings of the nucleus are completely occupied by agricultural fields. As a curiosity, there is a combat plane at the entrance of the town that represents a tribute to pioneer pilots of the Spanish state.

Architectural complex dating from the 10th century. Although for many years it was forgotten and its state was left in ruins, in 2007 a rehabilitation project was carried out to bring it back to life and give this location a social and cultural use for the inhabitants of Cevico Navero.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 128.5 km (80 miles) long and typically takes around 5 hours and 37 minutes to complete. This duration is for cycling time only and does not include stops for sightseeing or refreshments.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While much of the terrain in the El Cerrato region is described as 'long routes without steep slopes' and 'mostly well-paved surfaces and easy to ride,' the overall distance and elevation gain of 1177 meters (3862 feet) require good fitness. You can expect predominantly rolling or gently undulating landscapes, but be prepared for a challenging and rewarding ride.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is circular, starting and ending in Curiel del Duero. You can typically find parking in or near the village center of Curiel del Duero. As it's a rural area, street parking is often available, but always check local signs for restrictions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience quintessential Castilian landscapes, primarily featuring extensive agricultural fields, especially around Antigüedad. The route is rich in vineyards as it passes through the renowned Ribera del Duero wine region near Curiel del Duero. Expect rolling hills, historic villages, castles, and churches, offering varied perspectives of the beautiful Castilian countryside.

faqs.question: What notable sights or landmarks will I see on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical and cultural highlights. You'll pass by the impressive Curiel de Duero Castle, the Villafuerte de Esgueva Castle, and the Monastery of San Pelayo. Don't miss the unique Baltanás Underground Wine Cellars, an ethnological complex of traditional bodegas. You'll also cycle through the charming settlement of Antigüedad and see the Pesquera de Duero Weirs on the Duero River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is typically in spring (April-May) or autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally milder and more pleasant for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder temperatures of winter. The vineyards are also particularly beautiful in spring with new growth or in autumn during harvest.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While road cycling routes themselves don't typically have specific dog restrictions, this route primarily uses public roads. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or basket and that you adhere to all local regulations regarding pets in towns and any specific attractions you might visit along the way. Always carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads through the Ribera del Duero and El Cerrato regions. However, if you plan to visit specific castles, museums, or wineries along the way, they may have their own entrance fees or require reservations.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You will encounter parts of the Ruta del Duero and Ruta El Valle, as well as smaller segments of the Etapa 11: Roa – Peñafiel and Etapa 12: Peñafiel – Quintanilla de Onésimo.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several villages and towns like Curiel del Duero, Villafuerte de Esgueva, Baltanás, and Antigüedad, you will find opportunities for refreshments. These areas are known for their gastronomic and wine tourism, so look out for local cafes, restaurants, and bodegas where you can sample regional cuisine and wines.

faqs.question: What should I pack for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and appropriate cycling attire. Sunscreen, sunglasses, and a helmet are crucial. Consider bringing a light jacket, even in milder weather, as conditions can change. Don't forget your camera for the beautiful landscapes and historic sights!
